title: Why We Buy What We Do Not Need
source: AI生成·待校 · 经济与社会
intro: 为何我们总买不需要的东西？
paragraphs: Consumerism often drives us to purchase items we do not genuinely require. Advertisements skillfully link products to identity, suggesting that owning certain brands can enhance social status. This emotional appeal frequently overrides rational judgment, compelling individuals to buy impulsively. As a result, many accumulate possessions that neither satisfy real needs nor bring lasting happiness.

The mechanism behind this behavior involves psychological triggers. Marketers exploit the desire for belonging and self-esteem by portraying products as symbols of success. When consumers see peers with trendy gadgets, they experience social pressure to conform. This phenomenon, known as social comparison, activates impulsive buying. Furthermore, limited-time offers create a false sense of urgency, pushing people to act without careful thought.

However, this consumption pattern has negative consequences. Excessive spending leads to financial strain, debt accumulation, and clutter. Many regret their impulsive purchases once the initial excitement fades. Moreover, materialistic pursuits often fail to address deeper emotional needs, such as loneliness or insecurity. Instead of fostering genuine well-being, they perpetuate a cycle of temporary satisfaction followed by craving for more.

To cultivate rational consumption, one should pause before buying. Ask whether the item serves a real purpose or merely fills an emotional gap. Distinguishing between wants and needs is crucial. Additionally, individuals can focus on experiences rather than possessions, as research shows that experiential purchases yield greater happiness. By adopting mindful habits, we can break free from consumerist traps and achieve more sustainable fulfillment.
cn: 消费主义常常驱使我们购买并不真正需要的物品。广告巧妙地将产品与身份认同联系起来，暗示拥有某些品牌能提升社会地位。这种情感诉求往往压倒理性判断，迫使人们冲动购买。结果，许多人积累了大量既不能满足真实需求也不能带来持久幸福的物品。
这种行为背后的机制涉及心理触发因素。营销者利用人们对归属感和自尊的渴望，将产品描绘成成功的象征。当消费者看到同龄人拥有时髦的小玩意儿时，他们感受到从众的社会压力。这种现象被称为社会比较，会激发冲动购买。此外，限时优惠制造虚假的紧迫感，促使人们不经仔细思考就行动。
然而，这种消费模式有消极后果。过度支出导致财务紧张、债务累积和杂物堆积。一旦最初的兴奋消退，许多人会后悔自己的冲动购买。而且，物质追求往往无法满足更深层次的情感需求，如孤独或不安全感。它们非但不能促进真正的幸福，反而延续了一个由短暂满足和随后渴求组成的循环。
为了培养理性消费观，人们应在购买前暂停。询问物品是服务于实际目的还是仅仅填补情感空缺。区分需要和想要至关重要。此外，个人可以关注体验而非物品，因为研究表明体验型消费能带来更大的幸福。通过养成正念习惯，我们可以摆脱消费主义陷阱，实现更可持续的满足。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Consumerism helps people enhance their social status.
Advertising effectively meets consumers' genuine needs.
People buy unnecessary items due to psychological pressures and should adopt rational consumption.
questions.explain: 文章主旨是探讨消费主义如何导致人们购买不需要的东西，并提出培养理性消费观的建议。第一段提出现象，第二段分析心理机制，第三段指出问题，第四段给出解决方案。因此主旨应为「人们因心理压力购买不必要物品，应培养理性消费」。「消费主义提升社会地位」是广告的误导，并非主旨；「广告满足真实需求」与原文相反；「冲动购买带来长久幸福」也与第三段矛盾。

questions.q: According to the passage, how do advertisers influence consumers' purchasing decisions?
options: By creating emotional associations between products and identity.
By promoting products through scientific research.
By highlighting the practical functions of products.
By reducing the prices of products permanently.
questions.explain: 第一段明确提到「广告巧妙地将产品与身份认同联系起来」，第二段也指出营销者利用人们对归属感和自尊的渴望。因此广告通过建立产品与身份的情感关联来影响消费者。「强调实用功能」与原文情感策略相反；「永久降价」文中未提；「通过科学研究推广」无依据。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the 'social comparison' mentioned in paragraph 2?
options: It reduces the influence of advertisements on consumers.
It helps individuals express their unique identities.
It encourages consumers to make well-informed decisions.
It causes people to buy products they see others owning.
questions.explain: 第二段指出社会比较现象：当消费者看到同龄人拥有时髦物品时，感受到从众压力，从而激发冲动购买。因此可推断社会比较导致人们购买他人拥有的产品。「鼓励明智决策」与原文相反；「帮助表达独特身份」不对，社会比较导致从众而非追求独特；「减少广告影响」与原文矛盾，社会比较正是营销利用的机制。

questions.q: What does the word 'cultivate' in paragraph 4 most probably mean?
questions.explain: 第四段首句「To cultivate rational consumption」意为「培养理性消费观」，「cultivate」在此语境下是「培养、养成」的意思，与「develop」同义。「ignore」忽略、「criticize」批评、「abandon」放弃均不符合上下文。
